Gene Set Enrichment analysis part II: snRNAseq samples with metformin induced cardiac microtissue

Adapted from Gino Bonazza DCM samples analysis by Magdalena Czepiec and Maks Necki

Magdalena Czepiec
Department of Clinical Immunology / Faculty of Medicine / Jagiellonian University Medical College
GitHub


Maks Necki
Department of Clinical Immunology / Faculty of Medicine / Jagiellonian University Medical College
GitHub


1 Introduction

1.1 Setup: Libraries and Environement

# ---- Package lists ----
cran_packages <- c(
  "dplyr",
  "ggplot2",
  "patchwork",
  "htmltools",
  "tidyr",
  "Seurat",
  "stringr",
  "plotly",
  "kableExtra",
  "knitr",
  "sessioninfo"
)

bioc_packages <- c(
  "clusterProfiler",
  "org.Hs.eg.db",
  "enrichplot",
  "ReactomePA"
)

# ---- Function to install & load ----
install_and_load <- function(pkg, bioc = FALSE) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    if (bioc) {
      if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
        install.packages("BiocManager")
      }
      BiocManager::install(pkg, ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)
    } else {
      install.packages(pkg)
    }
  }
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}

# ---- Install & load CRAN packages ----
invisible(lapply(cran_packages, install_and_load))

# ---- Install & load Bioconductor packages ----
invisible(lapply(bioc_packages, install_and_load, bioc = TRUE))

cat("ALL PACKAGES SUCCESSFULLY LOADED")
## ALL PACKAGES SUCCESSFULLY LOADED

1.2 Importing files after GO BP Hierarchical Analysis (GSEA part I)

res_hierarchy_tgf_nc <- read.csv("C:/Users/magda/Desktop/Lib_11_MTs_GSEA/Skrypty do publikacji/GSEA part I (GO BP, KEGG, REACTOME, GO BP Hierarchy)/Cardiomyocytes/results_cardio_GO/deg_TGF_vs_NC_cardio_full.csv")
res_hierarchy_met_nc <- read.csv("C:/Users/magda/Desktop/Lib_11_MTs_GSEA/Skrypty do publikacji/GSEA part I (GO BP, KEGG, REACTOME, GO BP Hierarchy)/Cardiomyocytes/results_cardio_GO/deg_MET_vs_NC_cardio_full.csv")
res_hierarchy_mettgf_tgf <- read.csv("C:/Users/magda/Desktop/Lib_11_MTs_GSEA/Skrypty do publikacji/GSEA part I (GO BP, KEGG, REACTOME, GO BP Hierarchy)/Cardiomyocytes/results_cardio_GO/deg_MET_TGF_vs_TGF_cardio_full.csv")

1.3 Importing Seurat object containing analyzed and processed cardiomyocytes cells from all conditions

Cardio <- readRDS("C:/Users/magda/Desktop/Lib_11_MTs_GSEA/Skrypty do publikacji/GSEA part II (Gene Extraction)/Cardio.rds")

2 Genes from Pathways Extraction

The following function extracts genes driving enrichment signals from selected biological pathways and integrates them with differentially expressed genes to identify biologically relevant overlaps. By filtering pathways by category, expanding core enrichment gene sets, and mapping gene identifiers, it reconstructs pathway-level signals at the gene level. The subsequent intersection with DEG data highlights genes that are both statistically significant in expression changes and functionally embedded in enriched pathways. This allows prioritization of candidate genes that are not only differentially expressed but also mechanistically linked to specific biological processes. Such gene sets can then be used for downstream analyses like network construction, regulatory inference, biomarker discovery, or comparison of pathway activity across experimental conditions or cell types.

Main function for extraction analysis:

  run_pathway_gene_extraction <- function(
  gsea_df,
  deg_file,
  category = "Metabolism",
  output_name = "genes_of_interest"
  ) {
  
  if (is.null(gsea_df) || nrow(gsea_df) == 0) {
  return(list(genes_final = NULL, logs = "EMPTY INPUT"))
  }

  logs <- character()

  cat("\n=============================\n")
  cat("Processing category:", category, "\n")
  cat("=============================\n")
  
  # ---- Input Check ----
  if (is.null(gsea_df)) {
  message("Input GSEA dataframe is NULL - skipping")
  return(NULL)
  }
  
  # =========================================
  # 1. CATEGORY FILTER
  # =========================================
  # ---- Select only pathways belonging to chosen category ----
  df_category <- gsea_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(Category == category)

  if (nrow(df_category) == 0) {
  return(list(genes_final = NULL, logs = "EMPTY CATEGORY"))
  }
  
  logs <- c(logs, "\n[1] CATEGORY FILTER\n")
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Rows:", nrow(df_category), "\n"))
  
  # =========================================
  # 2. GENE EXPANSION
  # =========================================
  # ---- Split core_enrichment into individual genes ----
  df_split <- df_category %>%
    dplyr::mutate(core_enrichment = strsplit(core_enrichment, "/")) %>%
    tidyr::unnest(core_enrichment)
  
  logs <- c(logs, "\n[2] GENE EXPANSION\n")
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Rows:", nrow(df_split), "\n"))
  
  # =========================================
  # 3. GENE ID MAPPING
  # =========================================
  # ---- Convert ENTREZ IDs to gene SYMBOLS ----
  gene_map <- clusterProfiler::bitr(
    df_split$core_enrichment,
    fromType = "ENTREZID",
    toType   = "SYMBOL",
    OrgDb    = org.Hs.eg.db
  )
  
  logs <- c(logs, "\n[3] MAPPING\n")
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Mapped genes:", nrow(gene_map), "\n"))
  
  # ---- Merge mapping results ----
  df_mapped <- df_split %>%
    dplyr::left_join(gene_map, by = c("core_enrichment" = "ENTREZID"))
  
  logs <- c(logs, paste("After merge:", nrow(df_mapped),
      "| Missing SYMBOL:", sum(is.na(df_mapped$SYMBOL)), "\n"))
  
  # =========================================
  # 4. DATA CLEANING
  # =========================================
  # ---- Remove duplicates / NA genes ----
  df_clean <- df_mapped %>%
    dplyr::select(
      pathway = Description,
      gene    = SYMBOL,
      ENTREZID = core_enrichment,
      NES,
      p.adjust
    ) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(gene))
  
  logs <- c(logs, "\n[4] CLEANING\n")
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Rows:", nrow(df_clean), "\n"))
  
  # =========================================
  # 5. GSEA RESULTS FILTER
  # =========================================
  # ---- Significant genes (p.adjust and NES) ----
  df_filtered <- df_clean %>%
    dplyr::filter(p.adjust < 0.05, abs(NES) > 1)
  
  logs <- c(logs, "\n[5] GSEA FILTER\n")
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Rows:", nrow(df_filtered), "\n"))
  
  # ---- Remove duplicated genes ----
  df_unique <- df_filtered %>%
    dplyr::distinct(gene, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Unique genes:", nrow(df_unique), "\n"))
  
  # =========================================
  # 6. DEGs (INPUT AND FILTER)
  # ========================================= 
  deg <- read.csv(deg_file)
  
  logs <- c(logs, "\n[6] DEG INPUT\n")
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Rows:", nrow(deg), "\n"))
  
  # ---- Significant DEGs (logFC and FDR) ----
  deg_filtered <- deg %>%
    dplyr::filter(abs(logFC) > 1, FDR < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::select(gene, logFC, FDR)
  
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Filtered DEG:", nrow(deg_filtered), "\n"))
  
  # =========================================
  # 7. INTERSECTION GSEA AND DEGs RESULTS 
  # =========================================
  genes_final <- df_unique %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(deg_filtered, by = "gene") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(p.adjust)
  
  logs <- c(logs, "\n[7] INTERSECTION\n")
  logs <- c(logs, paste("Final genes:", nrow(genes_final), "\n"))
  
  # =========================================
  # 8. SAVING
  # =========================================
  gene_list <- genes_final$gene
  
  write.csv(
    gene_list,
    paste0(output_name, "_", category, ".csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  cat("\nSaved:", paste0(output_name, "_", category, ".csv"), "\n")
  
  # ---- Return Results ----
  return(list(
    df_category   = df_category,
    df_split      = df_split,
    gene_map      = gene_map,
    df_mapped     = df_mapped,
    df_clean      = df_clean,
    df_filtered   = df_filtered,
    df_unique     = df_unique,
    deg_filtered  = deg_filtered,
    genes_final   = genes_final,
    logs = logs
  ))
}

Helper function for plotting empty GSEA datasets:

make_empty_plot <- function(text = "No gene sets found") {
  ggplot() +
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 1),
      panel.background = element_rect(color = "white", fill = "white"),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
    ) +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.5,
      label = text,
      color = "black",
      size = 6,
      hjust = 0.5
    )
}

Function for plotting genes by sample:

  # ---- Plot ----
  plot_vln_batches <- function(
  seurat_obj,
  genes,
  group_by = "Sample",
  ncol = 2,
  nrow = 2,
  output_prefix = "VlnPlot",
  pt.size = 0.5,
  outdir = "results_cardio_GO"
  ) {

  seurat_obj$Sample <- factor(
  seurat_obj$Sample,
  levels = c("CM_NC", "CM_MET", "CM_TGF", "CM_MET_TGF")
  )

  if(!dir.exists(outdir)) dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE)

  # ---- Empty Plot ----
  if (is.null(genes) || length(genes) == 0) {
    message("No genes provided - returning empty plot")
  p <- make_empty_plot("No genes provided")

    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(outdir, paste0(output_prefix, "_EMPTY.png")),
      plot = p,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 150
    )

    return(list(empty_plot = p))
  }

  # ---- Remove duplicates / NA genes ----
  genes <- unique(genes)
  genes <- genes[!is.na(genes)]
  genes <- intersect(genes, rownames(seurat_obj))

  # ---- Empty Plot after removing duplicates / NA genes  ----
  if (length(genes) == 0) {
    message("No valid genes found in Seurat object")
  
  p <- make_empty_plot("No valid genes found")

  ggsave(
      filename = file.path(outdir, paste0(output_prefix, "_NO_VALID_GENES.png")),
      plot = p,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      dpi = 150
    )

    return(list(empty_plot = p))
  }

  batch_size <- ncol * nrow
  batches <- split(genes, ceiling(seq_along(genes) / batch_size))
  n_batches <- length(batches)
  n_genes <- length(genes)

  cat("Total genes:", n_genes, "\n")
  cat("Number of batches:", n_batches, "\n")

  plot_list <- list()

  # ---- Main Loop ----
  for (i in seq_along(batches)) {
  batch_genes <- batches[[i]]

  #cat("Plotting batch", i, "with", length(batch_genes), "genes\n")

  # ---- Empty batch ----
  if (length(batch_genes) == 0) {
  p <- make_empty_plot(paste0("Empty batch ", i))
  } else {
      p <- VlnPlot(
        seurat_obj,
        features = batch_genes,
        group.by = group_by,
        pt.size = pt.size,
        ncol = ncol,
        combine = TRUE
      ) + theme(legend.position = 'none')
    }

  # ---- Saving ----
    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(outdir, paste0(output_prefix, "_batch_", i, ".png")),
      plot = p,
      width = ncol * 12,
      height = nrow * 9,
      dpi = 150
    )
    plot_list[[paste0("batch_", i)]] <- p
  }
  return(plot_list)
  }

3 Showing extracted genes from pathways and from DEGs from each category for all comparisons across cardiomyocyte cluster

3.1 Metabolism Category

3.1.1 MET_TGF vs TGF

3.1.1.1 Gene Extraction

dir.create("results_cardio_extraction_metabolism", recursive = TRUE)

res_mettgf_tgf_metab <- run_pathway_gene_extraction(
  gsea_df = res_hierarchy_mettgf_tgf,
  deg_file = "C:/Users/magda/Desktop/Lib_11_MTs_GSEA/deg_MET_TGF_vs_TGF_cardio.csv",
  category = "Metabolism",
  output_name = file.path("results_cardio_extraction_metabolism", "mettgf_vs_tgf")
)
## 
## =============================
## Processing category: Metabolism 
## =============================
## 
## Saved: results_cardio_extraction_metabolism/mettgf_vs_tgf_Metabolism.csv

3.1.1.2 Intermediate QC Logs

cat(paste(unlist(res_mettgf_tgf_metab$logs), collapse = "\n"))
## 
## [1] CATEGORY FILTER
## 
## Rows: 7 
## 
## 
## [2] GENE EXPANSION
## 
## Rows: 489 
## 
## 
## [3] MAPPING
## 
## Mapped genes: 261 
## 
## After merge: 489 | Missing SYMBOL: 0 
## 
## 
## [4] CLEANING
## 
## Rows: 489 
## 
## 
## [5] GSEA FILTER
## 
## Rows: 489 
## 
## Unique genes: 261 
## 
## 
## [6] DEG INPUT
## 
## Rows: 6998 
## 
## Filtered DEG: 35 
## 
## 
## [7] INTERSECTION
## 
## Final genes: 4

3.1.1.3 Extracted Gene Violin Plots

plots_mettgf_tgf <- plot_vln_batches(
  seurat_obj = Cardio,
  genes = res_mettgf_tgf_metab$genes_final$gene,
  group_by = "Sample",
  ncol = 2,
  nrow = 2,
  output_prefix = "Cardio_Vln_metabolism_mettgf_tgf",
  outdir = "results_cardio_extraction_metabolism",
  pt.size = 0.5
)
## Total genes: 4 
## Number of batches: 1
invisible(lapply(plots_mettgf_tgf, print))

3.1.2 TGF vs NC

3.1.2.1 Gene Extraction

dir.create("results_cardio_extraction_metabolism", recursive = TRUE)

res_tgf_nc_metab <- run_pathway_gene_extraction(
  gsea_df = res_hierarchy_tgf_nc,
  deg_file = "C:/Users/magda/Desktop/Lib_11_MTs_GSEA/deg_TGF_vs_NC_cardio.csv",
  category = "Metabolism",
  output_name = file.path("results_cardio_extraction_metabolism", "tgf_vs_nc")
)

## =============================
## Processing category: Metabolism 
## =============================

## Saved: results_cardio_extraction_metabolism/tgf_vs_nc_Metabolism.csv

3.1.2.2 Intermediate QC Logs

cat(paste(unlist(res_tgf_nc_metab$logs), collapse = "\n"))
## 
## [1] CATEGORY FILTER
## 
## Rows: 5 
## 
## 
## [2] GENE EXPANSION

## Rows: 544 
## 
## 
## [3] MAPPING
## 
## Mapped genes: 440 
## 
## After merge: 544 | Missing SYMBOL: 0

## 
## [4] CLEANING
## 
## Rows: 544 
## 
##

## 
## Rows: 544 
## 
## Unique genes: 440 
## 
## 
## [6] DEG INPUT
## 
## Rows: 7282 
## 
## Filtered DEG: 75 
## 
## 
## [7] INTERSECTION

## Final genes: 2

3.1.2.3 Extracted Gene Violin Plots

plots_tgf_nc <- plot_vln_batches(
  seurat_obj = Cardio,
  genes = res_tgf_nc_metab$genes_final$gene,
  group_by = "Sample",
  ncol = 2,
  nrow = 2,
  output_prefix = "Cardio_Vln_metabolism_tgf_nc",

)

## Number of batches: 1

  pt.size = 0.5
)
## Total genes: 11 
## Number of batches: 3
invisible(lapply(plots_mettgf_tgf, print))

3.3.2 TGF vs NC

3.3.2.1 Gene Extraction

dir.create("results_cardio_extraction_signaling", recursive = TRUE)

res_tgf_nc_signaling <- run_pathway_gene_extraction(
  gsea_df = res_hierarchy_tgf_nc,
  deg_file = "C:/Users/magda/Desktop/Lib_11_MTs_GSEA/deg_TGF_vs_NC_cardio.csv",
  category = "Signaling",
  output_name = file.path("results_cardio_extraction_signaling", "tgf_vs_nc")
)
## 
## =============================
## Processing category: Signaling 
## =============================
## 
## Saved: results_cardio_extraction_signaling/tgf_vs_nc_Signaling.csv

3.3.2.2 Intermediate QC Logs

cat(paste(unlist(res_tgf_nc_signaling$logs), collapse = "\n"))
## 
## [1] CATEGORY FILTER
## 
## Rows: 10 
## 
## 
## [2] GENE EXPANSION
## 
## Rows: 365 
## 
## 
## [3] MAPPING
## 
## Mapped genes: 255 
## 
## After merge: 365 | Missing SYMBOL: 0 
## 
## 
## [4] CLEANING
## 
## Rows: 365 
## 
## 
## [5] GSEA FILTER
## 
## Rows: 365 
## 
## Unique genes: 255 
## 
## 
## [6] DEG INPUT
## 
## Rows: 7282 
## 
## Filtered DEG: 75 
## 
## 
## [7] INTERSECTION
## 
## Final genes: 15

3.3.2.3 Extracted Gene Violin Plots

plots_tgf_nc <- plot_vln_batches(
  seurat_obj = Cardio,
  genes = res_tgf_nc_signaling$genes_final$gene,
  group_by = "Sample",
  ncol = 2,
  nrow = 2,
  output_prefix = "Cardio_Vln_signaling_tgf_nc",
  outdir = "results_cardio_extraction_signaling",
  pt.size = 0.5
)
## Total genes: 15 
## Number of batches: 4
invisible(lapply(plots_tgf_nc, print))

3.7.3.3 Extracted Gene Violin Plots

plots_met_nc <- plot_vln_batches(
  seurat_obj = Cardio,
  genes = res_met_nc_development$genes_final$gene,
  group_by = "Sample",
  ncol = 2,
  nrow = 2,
  output_prefix = "Cardio_Vln_development_met_nc",
  outdir = "results_cardio_extraction_development",
  pt.size = 0.5
)
## Total genes: 4 
## Number of batches: 1
invisible(lapply(plots_met_nc, print))
